briefdescription.para: if object in a list can be deleted
if object destructor must call RecursiveRemove()
if object is referenced by a TRef or TRefArray
if object has a TUUID (its fUniqueID=UUIDNumber)
if object in a pad cannot be picked
if object does not want context menu
if object ctor succeeded but object should not be used
current cursor position
number of entries
raw data pointer
entry offsets pointer

detaileddescription.para: initial cursor position, used for calculating relative offsets
offset -> reference, used for pointer reading

memberdef.argsstring: (py::array_t< uint8_t > data, py::array_t< uint32_t > offsets, uint32_t initial_cursor_position)
memberdef.name: BinaryStream

briefdescription.para: Construct a BinaryStream from numpy arrays.

parameterdescription.para: A numpy array of uint8_t containing the raw data.
A numpy array of uint32_t containing the offsets for each entry.

briefdescription.para: Read a value of type T from the stream, handling endianness.

simplesect.para: The value read from the stream.

briefdescription.para: Read the fVersion field from the stream.
Read the fNBytes field from the stream, checking the byte count mask.

simplesect.para: The fNBytes value without the mask.

parameterdescription.para: if the byte count mask is not set.

briefdescription.para: Read a null-terminated (\0) string from the stream.

simplesect.para: The string read from the stream.

briefdescription.para: Read an object's header from the stream. The object header has fNBytes, fVersion, fTag. If fTag == kNewClassTag, then a null-terminated class name follows.

simplesect.para: The class name if the object is a new class, empty string otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Read a TString from the stream. A TString has length (uint8_t). If length == 255, then the length is a following uint32_t. Then following length bytes of string data.

simplesect.para: The TString data read from the stream, as a std::string.

briefdescription.para: Skip n bytes in the stream.
Skip the fNBytes field. Equivalent to read_fNBytes() but does not return the value, since the mask need to be checked.
Skip the fVersion field.
Skip a null-terminated (\0) string in the stream.
Skip an object's header in the stream. The object header has fNBytes, fVersion, fTag. If fTag == kNewClassTag, then a null-terminated class name follows.
Skip a TObject in the stream. A TObject has fVersion (2 bytes), fUniqueID (4 bytes), fBits (4 bytes). If fBits & kIsReferenced, then a pidf (2 bytes) follows.
Get the raw data pointer.
Get the current cursor pointer.
Get the entry offsets array pointer.
Get the index object.
Get the initial cursor offset, which is used for calculating relative offsets.
Get the references map, which is used for pointer reading.
Get the number of entries.
Debug print the next n bytes from the current cursor.
